mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through the market.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand and rising corporate revenue expectations, driving positive equity valuations and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as momentum traders react to demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 507533.0 million dollars as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign for > 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side productivity gains, which support profit margins and equity valuations. Sustained INDPRO growth over 3 months predicts positive 20-day forward returns as supply-side strength becomes priced into earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts and retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand across both residential and consumer sectors. This interaction predicts negative 60-day forward returns as forward-looking equity markets price in reduced economic growth and corporate earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% and RSAFS growth < 0% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1173.0 thousand units as of 2024-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth and declining unemployment rate jointly signal tight labor market conditions that raise wage pressures and unit labor costs. This interaction predicts negative 20-day forward returns as markets reprice inflation expectations and Fed rate path upward.
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.5% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2.5% indicates real rates remain negative, creating financial repression that supports equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns only when real rates are negative; it reverses to negative returns when real rates turn positive.
regime_hint: negative real rates with financial repression
failure_condition: Direction reverses (negative returns dominate) when real rates turn positive for > 3 consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in the negative real rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 decelerating below 1.5% annualized rate signals late-cycle slowdown and earnings recession risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns in late-cycle expansion regimes but becomes neutral or positive in early-cycle recovery regimes where deceleration is expected and already priced.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 1.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18507.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2023-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances in late-cycle regimes, or mechanism direction flips without corresponding regime shift in UNRATE or FEDFUNDS, indicating regime classification failure.
